text: If local anesthesia is administered and the patient has a high tolerance to anesthesia, the patient must be asked every 20 minutes if they are experiencing any pain.
If local anesthesia is administered and the patient has a high tolerance to anesthesia, additional anesthesia must be administered every 20 minutes
Before a surgical intervention, the nurse must obtain the patient’s consent. After obtaining consent, the nurse must conduct a ``time out'' procedure
Before a surgical intervention, the nurse must obtain the patient’s consent. After obtaining consent, the doctor must conduct a ``time out'' procedure.
If the patient is over 65, a nurse must approve discharge before the patient is discharged, and approval must occur within 24 hours prior to discharge. The rule is valid from 2024 to 2030.
If the patient is over 65 or has more than 5 medications, a doctor must approve discharge before the patient is discharged. The rule is valid from 2025 to 2031.
Before a surgery, blood has to be analyzed.
If the doctor does not authorize skipping blood analysis, the analysis test should happen before the surgery.
Physical examination and blood test are required before making a diagnosis.
A diagnosis may be made even if the physical examination and blood test have not been performed.
Blood samples must be collected from all patients.
If the patient is an adult, blood samples must be collected.
Immediately after preparing the patient, anesthesia must be applied.
Anesthesia should be applied after patient preparation, but the timing is flexible and not restricted to immediately after.
